idea: An app that detects crop diseases using AI and suggests fertilizers
feedback: This is a highly impactful idea, but mechanically it is extremely overdone at hackathons. Every event has a crop disease detector using transfer learning on ResNet. To stand out, you need a twist—such as offline-first capability for remote farmers using Web-Assembly, or SMS-based fallback.
mvpFeatures: Camera integration with offline caching (Service Workers)
On-device inference using a quantized TensorFlow Lite model
SMS-based fallback lookup via Twilio API

idea: A blockchain-powered social network for exchanging homemade recipes
feedback: You are forcing blockchain into a problem that doesn't need it. Writing smart contracts and handling wallet authentication for recipes will consume 90% of your 24 hours. The impact is also low. Pivot to a standard Web2 stack and focus on an AI feature, like generating recipes from pantry photos.
mvpFeatures: LLM integration to generate 3 recipe options based on ingredients

idea: A VS Code extension that generates unit tests based on Git diffs before committing
feedback: Excellent utility with high developer appeal. The risk here is dealing with the VS Code Extension API learning curve within 24 hours. Keep the scope painfully small: only support one language (e.g., JavaScript) and only trigger on explicitly selected diffs.
mvpFeatures: VS Code command pallet trigger reading the current active Git diff
LLM prompt to generate Jest tests based on the diff
Automatic insertion of generated tests into a .test.js file
techStack: Anthropic Claude 3 Haiku API (for low latency)

idea: An AR navigation app for visually impaired people using LiDAR on iPhones
feedback: Massive impact, but a 24-hour death trap. Native iOS, ARKit, LiDAR processing, and accessible audio queues involve too many complex layers. You won't finish a demo. Pivot to an audio-only assistant that describes static photos taken by the user instead of doing real-time AR.
mvpFeatures: Voice command to trigger photo capture
Vision API to generate a dense semantic description of the scene
Text-to-Speech (TTS) readout of the scene description

idea: A browser extension that automatically summarizes lengthy terms of service into a 'danger score'
feedback: A classic, solid hackathon project. It's technically very feasible because it's just scraping the DOM and making a single LLM call. The challenge is making a beautiful UI panel. Focus entirely on the visualization of the 'danger score' to wow the judges.
mvpFeatures: Content script to scrape TOS text from the active page
LLM endpoint to extract 'red flag' clauses and output a 1-100 score
Popup UI displaying the gauge chart and 3 bullet points of risky clauses
techStack: Tailwind CSS + Radix UI (for polished popup components)
